observations.note: All 12 integration tests PASSED (100% success rate). Zero component interaction failures detected.

Dual-path architecture verified: source file at src/claude/skills/designing-systems/references/domain-reference-generation.md is in sync with operational path .claude/skills/designing-systems/references/domain-reference-generation.md

No conflicts detected between domain-reference-generation.md reference file and designing-systems SKILL.md. Reference file is loaded on-demand (Phase 5.7), not inline in skill definition. Architecture follows progressive disclosure pattern (ADR-012).

All 4 heuristic definitions (DH-01 through DH-04) verified present with correct target agents: DH-01→backend-architect, DH-02→test-automator, DH-03→security-auditor, DH-04→code-reviewer. Each heuristic has documented trigger conditions, source files, and evaluation pseudocode.

Reference file template includes all 5 required sections: (1) When to Load, (2) Domain Constraints, (3) Forbidden Patterns, (4) Language Patterns, (5) Build Commands. Auto-generation header contains DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY, source files list, generation date format (YYYY-MM-DD), and /audit-alignment --generate-refs regeneration command.

Heuristic evaluation constraint verified: only Read() and Grep() tools documented as permitted. No Write/Edit operations during heuristic evaluation. File explicitly documents read-only behavior requirement (BR-001 business rule).

Structured output format verified for triggered heuristics: returns heuristic ID (DH-01..DH-04), target agent name, output file path (.claude/agents/{agent}/references/project-{type}.md), and list of source context files. Skip signal documented: empty list with 'No domain references needed' message when no heuristics trigger.

Heuristic thresholds verified to use strict greater-than operators: DH-03 uses >5 headings (not >=5), DH-02 uses >1 language (not >=1), DH-04 uses 2+ languages (>=2). Boundary value behavior documented correctly to prevent false positives on simple projects.

Derivation purity constraint documented (AC#11, BR-002): all generated reference file content must be 100% context-derived. No hardcoded knowledge permitted. No synthesis or hallucination. File explicitly documents this critical constraint.

Naming convention verified: project-*.md pattern documented with mapping table. Examples given: project-domain.md (backend-architect), project-testing.md (test-automator), project-security.md (security-auditor), project-review.md (code-reviewer). Full paths documented: .claude/agents/{agent-name}/references/project-{type}.md

Integration test suite coverage: 12/12 test suites passed. Tests validate: 4 heuristic definitions (AC#1), 4 individual heuristic triggers (AC#2-5), read-only constraint (AC#6), structured output (AC#7-8), template header (AC#9), template sections (AC#10), derivation purity (AC#11), naming convention (AC#12). 100% acceptance criteria coverage.

Markdown documentation file - coverage thresholds (95/85/80) do NOT apply. No executable code present. Integration validation focused on: (1) dual-path sync, (2) component interaction via reference loading, (3) heuristic definition correctness, (4) template specification completeness, (5) test suite pass rate. All criteria MET.
